body.title: [triage] TesterDevice returned counts:{} and a RED verdict in the same breath — a parse failure and a real result are the same shape, and I nearly reported the number I never measured.
body.intention: I intend that the tester never emits a verdict it cannot substantiate — a run whose results could not be PARSED is INDETERMINATE, exactly like a run whose seal could not be CONFIRMED, and INDETERMINATE is never GREEN and never a bare RED. [intention_date: 2026-07-14]
body.description:
**MEASURED 2026-07-14.** A full-suite run under the router returned:

    verdict     : RED | policy_holds: True
    counts      : {}                     <-- the _COUNTS regex matched NOTHING
    Hex reaches : 364 (kernel-counted)

The device happily emitted **RED with no idea how many tests passed or failed.** The regex found no
pytest summary line (extra `-q --tb=no` flags in test_paths changed the tail's shape), so it returned an
empty dict — and an empty dict is falsy, and nothing checked it.

**THE DEFECT IS THE SHAPE, NOT THE REGEX.** `counts == {}` means 'I could not parse the result'. It is
currently indistinguishable from a legitimate result, and it rides alongside a confident RED. That is the
day's recurring bug once more: **two causes, one signal.** The tester already gets this right for the SEAL
(a seal it cannot confirm => INDETERMINATE, never GREEN). It does not apply its own rule to its RESULTS.

**WHAT IT COST:** I was one sentence away from telling Akien the 716 peer-auth failures were fixed, on the
strength of a run that had not told me anything. I caught it only because I had said out loud that I would
not report a number I had not measured. **A discipline I have to remember is not a property of the system.**

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/tester/device.py (_COUNTS, run_tests verdict assembly).

**Scope boundary:** the verdict logic. Do not widen this into general tester hardening.

**Completion criteria:** an unparseable result yields INDETERMINATE with a reason naming WHY (no summary
line found, tail attached), `passed` stays None, and no GREEN or bare RED is ever emitted without counts.

**Test plan:** red->green — feed run_tests output with no pytest summary line; RED: it returns RED with
counts:{}. GREEN: it returns INDETERMINATE with the reason and the tail. The hollow build this must fail:
making the regex more permissive. A better regex still leaves the failure mode — the point is that the
UNPARSEABLE CASE MUST BE A DISTINCT SIGNAL, not a rarer one.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
